sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 21:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 21:1
items.html: The record of phase four, which is the male of Keter, is only half a record, its weaker half, which is only the phase of clothing. However, the record of phase three is complete with two phases of coarseness: of clothing and of extension. The male becomes needy of the female, to the extent that there is equivalence between them, and they become ZON of a single degree. (Inner Light, Item‎ 6)
